title: Fallout 76 AutoTestClient Bot Framework - Deep Analysis
summary: Bethesda shipped a comprehensive automated testing and bot framework inside the Fallout 76 game files as Papyrus scripts under the AutoTestClient namespace. These scripts extend the Player or Actor base class and use native engine functions (SetAIDriven, PathToReference, FindRandomCombatTarget, etc.) to simulate full player behavior: navigation, combat, looting, interior transitions, weapon testing, chem usage, quest progression, and Nuclear Winter (Babylon) bot behavior. The framework includes a client distribution system that spreads bots across the map, a shared assertion/logging API in Debug.psc, and a network message (DebugGameplayAutoTestComplete) for reporting test results to the server.

base_classes.Player: Engine-native class (player.psc) that exposes SetAIDriven(), GetPlayerControls(), SetWantSprinting(), PathToReference(), FindRandomCombatTarget(), FindClosestReferenceOfType(), FindRandomReferenceWithKeyword(), IsInsideStormZone(), GetHeadingToStormZoneCenter(), HasSpawnedIntoBabylonGame(), FastTravel(), MoveTo(), and all standard Actor methods.
base_classes.Actor: Standard Creation Engine actor class with combat, pathfinding, inventory, and state machine APIs.

architecture.namespace: AutoTestClient (client-side bots) and AutoTestServer/AutoTestShared (server-side tests and shared utilities)
architecture.entry_point: Each script has a StartTest() function, called by the engine's autotest system. Tests report completion via Debug.AutoTestComplete(bool).
architecture.client_distribution: AutoTestClient:Common.DistributeClients() teleports each bot client to one of 24 predefined world cell coordinates using CowToCell(), spreading load across the map.
architecture.test_completion: DebugGameplayAutoTestComplete network message (single bool: m_succeeded) sent from client to server when a test finishes.
architecture.server76_integration: The Server76 private server emulator has the DebugGameplayAutoTestComplete message type defined, the BabylonFacadeComponent includes a botVal field (3 bits), and the BabylonManager initializes Nuclear Winter match state. No reimplementation of SetAIDriven/PathToReference was found - those are engine-native functions.

navigation.SetAIDriven(bool): CRITICAL: Native function on Player that switches the player character to AI-controlled mode. When true, the engine's NPC AI system drives the player character, enabling pathfinding and autonomous movement.
navigation.PathToReference(ObjectReference, float): Native function that makes the AI-driven player pathfind to a target reference using the engine's navmesh system. Returns bool for success/failure. Used with retry loops (typically 3-5 attempts).
navigation.SetWantSprinting(bool): Makes the AI-driven player sprint while navigating.
navigation.MoveTo(ObjectReference, float, float, fl: Instant teleport to a reference position.
navigation.GetPlayerControls(): Checks if player has manual control (used to guard SetAIDriven calls).
navigation.Debug.CenterOnWorldAndWait(string, int,: Teleport to world coordinates and wait for load.
navigation.Debug.PlayerGoTo(string): Navigate to a string key reference.

combat.FindRandomCombatTarget(float): Finds a random hostile actor within radius. Used by GrindMonsters and MoveAndFight scripts.

interaction.Activate(ObjectReference, bool): Activates doors, containers, flora, and other interactables.
interaction.FindClosestReferenceOfType(Form, float): Finds nearest reference of a given form type.
interaction.FindAllReferencesOfType(Form, float): Gets array of all references of type in radius.
interaction.Debug.FindRandomReferenceWithFormTypeId(: Finds random reference by form type ID.

data_collection.Debug.GetNormalizedDamageResistance(Acto: Gets normalized DR for an actor.
data_collection.Debug.GetNormalizedWeaponsDamageForActor: Gets array of weapon damage values.
data_collection.Debug.GetWeaponsInActorInventory(Actor): Gets array of weapons an actor has.
data_collection.Debug.GetInstanceLevelOfPlayerWeapons(Ac: Gets instance levels of player weapons.
data_collection.Debug.GetFormsByType(int): Gets ALL forms of a given type - can enumerate every item, NPC, weapon, etc.
data_collection.Debug.GetNumMapMarkers(): Gets total number of map markers.
data_collection.Debug.AutoTestReadFile(string): Reads a text file from disk (batch files, config, etc).
data_collection.Debug.DBSendPlayerPosition(): Sends player position to a database.
data_collection.Debug.QuestGetInstanceIDs(string): Gets quest instance IDs by name.

assertions.Debug.AutoTestComplete(bool): Marks test as complete with pass/fail.
assertions.Debug.AutoTestGetExecutionIndex(): Gets bot client index (for multi-bot distribution).

nuclear_winter_babylon.IsInsideStormZone(float): Checks if player is inside the NW storm zone with extra radius buffer.
nuclear_winter_babylon.GetHeadingToStormZoneCenter(): Gets heading angle toward storm safe zone center.
nuclear_winter_babylon.HasSpawnedIntoBabylonGame(): Checks if player has spawned into a NW match.
nuclear_winter_babylon.Debug.BabylonBotShouldLoot(): Native bool: should the NW bot go loot right now.
nuclear_winter_babylon.Debug.BabylonBotShouldShoot(): Native bool: should the NW bot shoot right now.
nuclear_winter_babylon.Debug.BabylonBotShouldMove(): Native bool: should the NW bot move right now.
nuclear_winter_babylon.SpawnPickRandomPoint(): Picks random spawn point on NW map.

world_manipulation.Debug.TestAllCells(int, int, int, int, s: Tests all cells from a file list.
world_manipulation.Debug.QueueBatchedServerConsoleCommand(s: Queues console commands for batch execution.

core_behavior_bots.purpose: World exploration bot - visits 24 named locations by pathfinding to map markers, enters/exits interior doors
behavior: Distributes clients across map via Common.DistributeClients()
Sets player to AI-driven mode with sprinting

Loops forever through 24 locations: VTecAG, Sugar Grove, Aaronholt Homestead, Abandoned Bog Town, Allegheny Asylum, AMS Corporate HQ, Appalachian Antiques, Arktos Pharma, AVR Medical Center, Beckley, Berkeley, Blackwater Mine, Burning Mine, Camden Park, Camp McClintock, Clarksburg, DemoStart, Dolly Sods, Eastern Regional Pen, Freddy Fears, Giant Teapot, Grafton, Harpers Ferry, Garrahan Estate
For each location: PathToReference with up to 5 retries, then enters/exits each interior door pair
Interior visits: pathfind to door, activate it, wait 150 seconds inside, activate exit door, wait 40 seconds
Randomly selects destination each loop iteration
core_behavior_bots.data_collected: Pathfinding success/failure per location, door interaction reliability, load time between cells

core_behavior_bots.purpose: Loot collection bot - finds and loots containers or harvests flora within 10000 unit radius
behavior: Distributes clients, sets timer for test duration
Sets AI-driven with sprinting
Alternates between container looting and flora harvesting based on execution parameter
Container mode: finds nearest container (form type 33), pathfinds to it, waits, clears inventory
Flora mode: finds nearest flora (form type 46), pathfinds to it, activates (harvests), waits, clears inventory
CollectionRadius of 10000 units for search area
Clears inventory after each collection to prevent weight overload
core_behavior_bots.data_collected: Container/flora density and distribution, pathfinding coverage, loot availability patterns

core_behavior_bots.purpose: Combat grinding bot - finds and fights random enemies in a 10000 unit radius
behavior: Loads ammo form (ID 127606 = likely 5mm or .308) from SeventySix.esm
Fills ammo inventory
Distributes clients, waits 10 seconds for world to settle
Sets AI-driven with sprinting
Main loop: FindRandomCombatTarget at 10000 range, pathfind to target, engage in combat
Fight function: disables AI-driven mode (transfers to engine combat AI), polls target death for up to 100 ticks (1 second), refills ammo, re-enables AI-driven
The AI-driven toggle during combat is critical: SetAIDriven(false) lets the engine's combat AI take over for actual fighting, then SetAIDriven(true) resumes pathfinding control
core_behavior_bots.data_collected: Enemy spawn density, combat encounter rates, time-to-kill metrics, pathfinding-to-combat transitions

nuclear_winter_bot.purpose: Nuclear Winter (codename 'Babylon') battle royale bot - simulates a player in NW matches with firing, looting, and storm zone awareness
behavior: Waits random delay, selects one of 12 weapon loadouts by index
Each weapon has hex form IDs for weapon + ammo + count (100 rounds for most, 10 for heavy weapons)
Gets BabylonBagKeyword (form 5307850) for finding NW loot bags
Starts two timer systems: fire timer (1.9-2.1s interval) and loot timer (29-31s interval)
Movement: checks if inside storm zone (500 unit buffer), moves toward center if outside, alternates left/right flanking if still outside, random heading if safe
Fire timer: polls Debug.BabylonBotShouldShoot(), decrements reload counter, reloads when empty
Loot timer: polls Debug.BabylonBotShouldLoot(), finds random reference with BabylonBagKeyword within 5000 units, teleports to bag
Movement controlled by Debug.BabylonBotShouldMove() native function
NOTE: The main movement While loop has 'While false' - likely decompiler artifact or the movement was controlled entirely by native BabylonBot functions
nuclear_winter_bot.data_collected: NW match flow, storm zone behavior, loot bag distribution, weapon cycling, combat timing
nuclear_winter_bot.notable: The 3 Debug.BabylonBot* native functions indicate the engine has a C++ layer that makes tactical decisions about when bots should shoot, loot, or move. The Papyrus script is just the execution layer - the actual AI decision-making happens in native code.

movement_bots.purpose: Moves in a heading direction with a serpentine pattern, fights any enemy encountered within 1000 units
behavior: Distributes clients, gets random heading and test duration
Movement pattern: move 5s forward, 3s right, 5s forward, 3s left (serpentine)
Between each move segment, checks for combat targets at 1000 range
If target found: engages until dead, then resumes movement
Fight is blocking: polls IsDead() every 10ms

movement_bots.purpose: Simple circular movement pattern for stress testing - moves in 4 directions for 5s each
movement_bots.behavior: Loops four 5-second movement segments until timer expires. Likely 4 cardinal directions creating a rough circle.

movement_bots.purpose: Straight-line movement with serpentine: 5s forward, 2s sideways, repeat
Long-duration movement soak test - moves for 10 minutes then teleports back to start and repeats
movement_bots.behavior: Every 600 seconds, teleports back to a starting position with a new random heading. Runs indefinitely. Used for stability/memory leak testing.

movement_bots.purpose: Same as MoveInDirection but distributes clients first
Fixed heading movement with client distribution
Pathfinds to map markers sequentially using AI-driven mode with up to 5 retries per marker
movement_bots.behavior: Gets a list of markers, optionally teleports to a start position, then pathfinds to each marker in order. Uses CheckAndSetPlayerAIDriven/CheckAndClearPlayerAIDriven pattern.

movement_bots.purpose: Simple pathfinding test - navigates to a single reference (form 80769) with 3 retries

cell_transition_bots.purpose: Distributes bots into 24 interior cells including major dungeons, missile silos, and vaults
Stress-tests interior/exterior cell transitions by entering and exiting a specific door 3 times using state machine
cell_transition_bots.behavior: Uses Papyrus state machine (transitionedToInterior/transitionedToExterior) and RegisterForPlayerTeleport events. Tests door forms 396052 (interior) and 396049 (exterior).

cell_transition_bots.purpose: Repeatedly visits a specific interior (door pair 4103764/4103721) until timer expires
CenterOnCell stress test - teleports through the 24 predefined cells sequentially
Tests player shelters - creates CAMP, enters shelter vaults (Reception, Atrium, Server Room, Living Quarters)
cell_transition_bots.notable: Contains 26 encoded test location strings (hex coordinate data) for placing CAMPs at different map positions

cell_transition_bots.purpose: Cycles through all shelter types (Reception -> Atrium -> Server Room -> Exit) infinitely

weapon_and_combat_tests.purpose: Tests equipping and firing 17 different weapons, verifying ammo consumption
weapon_and_combat_tests.behavior: For each weapon: adds weapon+ammo via console, equips, fires 150 shots (0.1s interval), checks ammo count decreased. Tests: 10mm Pistol, Hunting Rifle, Combat Shotgun, Laser Rifle, Plasma Gun, Gatling Laser, Fat Man, Minigun, Assault Rifle, Missile Launcher, Broadsider, .44 Revolver, etc.
weapon_and_combat_tests.data_collected: Weapon fire rate validation, ammo consumption accuracy

weapon_and_combat_tests.purpose: Comprehensive damage testing - spawns weapons and creatures, measures damage per hit and hits-to-kill
weapon_and_combat_tests.behavior: For each weapon: spawns creature, measures HP before/after a shot, calculates damage and hits-to-kill. Reads creature list from file. Provides weapon-vs-creature damage matrix data.
weapon_and_combat_tests.data_collected: Damage per hit, hits to kill, weapon instance levels, creature HP - a complete damage balance dataset

weapon_and_combat_tests.purpose: Simple combat wait test - distributes clients and waits 180 seconds
Spawns mass combat scenarios: Rats vs Brotherhood of Steel, Raiders vs Deathclaw, Robot Fight. Repeats 3 times with 40s intervals.
weapon_and_combat_tests.data_collected: Server performance under mass NPC combat load

weapon_and_combat_tests.purpose: Tests explosion effects - reads explosion form names and triggers them sequentially
Spawns a Molerat, equips weapon (form 974060), initiates combat via StartCombat(), registers for OnDying event
weapon_and_combat_tests.notable: Uses RegisterForRemoteEvent on the target's OnDying event - demonstrates event-driven combat AI

weapon_and_combat_tests.purpose: Iterates through NPC forms (type 50) and kills them

item_and_stat_tests.purpose: Spawns every item in the game by form type - ARMO, BOOK, INGR, MISC, CNCY, WEAP, AMMO, KEYM, NOTE, PPAK, PACH, LVLI, ARMA, OTFT, COBJ, OMOD
item_and_stat_tests.data_collected: Complete item database enumeration - uses Debug.GetFormsByType() to get ALL forms of each type
item_and_stat_tests.notable: This is essentially a complete game database dump tool. It enumerates every armor, weapon, ammo, key, note, outfit, recipe, and mod in the game.

item_and_stat_tests.purpose: Spawns and verifies 6 creature types: LvlRadtoad, LvlMolerat, LvlScorchedRanged, LvlBloatfly, LvlReaperBot, LvlMirelurkCrab
item_and_stat_tests.behavior: Spawns each creature, waits, then finds nearest reference of that type within 2000 units to verify spawn worked

item_and_stat_tests.purpose: Spawns creatures and extracts full stat blocks: DR, ER, Fire/Poison/Cryo/Rad resistance, MaxHP, unarmed damage, weapon inventory, weapon damage values
item_and_stat_tests.data_collected: Complete creature stat database - normalized resistances, HP, damage values, weapon inventories. Reads creature list from file, so any creature can be profiled.
item_and_stat_tests.notable: This is a data mining goldmine. It can generate a complete creature stats spreadsheet for every enemy in the game.

item_and_stat_tests.purpose: Dumps all weapons in player inventory with damage values and instance levels
Iterates through every armor form in the game (type 31) and attempts to equip each one
Comprehensive chem testing - uses every chem and verifies actor value changes
item_and_stat_tests.data_collected: Exact actor value changes per chem, perk grants, verification of game balance data

item_and_stat_tests.purpose: Tests healing items: Stimpak, Super Stimpak, Diluted Stimpak, Healing Salve, Blood Pack
Tests entering and exiting power armor using state machine (waitForEnter/waitForExit/done) with OnSit/OnGetUp events

quest_tests.purpose: Runs through 9 smoke test quests (TestSmokeQuest01-10) by teleporting to quest locations and checking instance IDs
Distributes bot clients across 5 different quests simultaneously for parallel quest testing
Load testing - teleports to quest location, sets quest stage via server console commands
Vault 94 raid load test - teleports in, moves and fights in serpentine pattern for 10 minutes, teleports out, repeats
quest_tests.behavior: Vault run timer of 600s, test timer of 120s. Serpentine movement with combat checks. Tests Vault 94 raid stability.

distribution_and_infrastructure.purpose: Shared utilities - DistributeClients() sends bot clients to 24 predefined cell coordinates spread across the map
distribution_and_infrastructure.coordinates: 24 (x,y) cell coordinate pairs covering the full Appalachia map from (-43,-37) to (43,35)

distribution_and_infrastructure.purpose: Distributes 32 clients in a grid pattern for maximum server load testing, including a dense cluster near (0,0)
Advanced distribution - reads cell names from a file, converts to world coordinates, uses Ulam spiral algorithm for overflow placement
distribution_and_infrastructure.notable: Implements a spiral coordinate algorithm to place excess clients that don't fit in predefined cells. Shows sophisticated test infrastructure.

distribution_and_infrastructure.purpose: Shared test utilities: CreateDummyPlayer(), SetGameHour(), GetGlobalByFormId(), GetActorValueByFormId(), GetPerkCardByFormId()
Simply waits for a specified duration - used as a baseline/control test
Core debug/test API exposing 60+ native functions for testing, assertions, world manipulation, data extraction, and profiling

graphics_and_performance_tests.purpose: TAC (Test All Cells) standard test - empty, likely triggers native TestAllCells
Distributed TAC test - assigns each of 24 clients a different cell set
Graphics rendering test across all cells
Limited graphics test using BatchFiles/tac11cells.txt cell list

other_tests.purpose: Verifies SPECIAL actor values exist and are > 0, tests SetActorValue server authority
Tests the Debug assertion functions themselves
Tests game hour manipulation and wrapping (0, 12, 30, -30 hours)
Server-side perk card testing (empty - likely native)
SPECIAL bonus perk card testing (empty - likely native)
Time-of-day dependent perk card testing (empty - likely native)
Tests networked keyword scripting by activating 9 buttons and counting enabled baseball statue references
Developer test quest (V. Harbison) for a robot rager event with bot behavior timers, destruction objectives, frenzy effects, and NPC wave spawning
other_tests.notable: Named after a specific Bethesda developer (V. Harbison). Contains BotBehavior timer and scenes with Nice Bots, Barking Bots, and attack waves.

autotest_network_message.description: Network message for reporting autotest completion. Contains single bool m_succeeded. Shows the client-server protocol for test result reporting.

babylon_bot_support.description: The QuickPlayBabylonFacadeComponent has a botVal field (3 bits, 0-7) that tracks bot status in Nuclear Winter matches. This is the server-side representation of NW bots.

babylon_manager.description: Initializes Nuclear Winter match infrastructure with storm zones, map voting, spawn pickers, team management, and nuke scheduling.

server76_findings.native_functions_not_reimplemented: SetAIDriven, PathToReference, FindRandomCombatTarget, and other bot-critical native functions are NOT found in Server76's PapyrusVMNode. These are engine-native C++ functions that only exist in Bethesda's official server binary.

ai_training_potential.assessment: HIGH - This bot framework represents a production-quality automated testing and data collection system that could be repurposed or studied for AI training in several ways.

data_collection_capabilities.description: Bots systematically visit every named location, interior, and map marker in the game. This generates complete navmesh traversal data and location connectivity graphs.
Bots encounter, fight, and track enemies across the world. DamageTest generates weapon-vs-creature damage matrices. SpawnCreaturesAndGetStats extracts full stat blocks for every creature.
Debug.GetFormsByType() can enumerate EVERY form of any type in the game. Combined with stat extraction, this creates a complete game database.
These scripts verify exact numeric effects of every consumable, weapon damage values, and ammo consumption rates.
Long-running soak tests and distributed load tests with 24-32 simultaneous bots generate server performance baselines.
The NW bot demonstrates a complete BR decision loop: storm zone awareness, looting, firing, movement. The native BabylonBotShould* functions suggest deeper C++ AI logic.

key_native_apis_for_ai: SetAIDriven(bool) - The master switch for autonomous player control
PathToReference() - Navmesh-based pathfinding from player perspective
FindRandomCombatTarget() - Enemy detection and target selection
Debug.AutoTestReadFile() - External data input for parameterized tests
implications_for_gamecryptids: The ExploreWorld script's location list provides a curated list of 24 key locations to investigate for secrets
MoveToInteriorDistributed lists 24 interior cells including hidden areas like WhitespringGroundsHoldingCell and Vault63
The pathfinding retry system (3-5 attempts) reveals areas that are hard to reach or have navmesh issues - potential secret areas
Debug.GetFormsByType() could enumerate every MISC, NOTE, TERM, and BOOK form to find hidden lore
The chem testing reveals exact stat values that could identify hidden or unused chems
SpawnCreaturesAndGetStats could profile every creature to find anomalous stat blocks suggesting unused/hidden enemies
The shelter test location strings contain encoded coordinate data for every testable map position
